headline: She survived the bushfires. It was the supermarket price that nearly broke her.
subhead: Rachel Ward rebuilt her Kempsey cattle farm with regenerative practices. Then she found a better way to get her beef to the families who want it.
hero_copy:
In 2020, fire tore through Rachel Ward's property on the northern NSW coast. She lost fences, pasture, years of work. But the cattle survived. She rebuilt.

What nearly finished her was not the fire. It was what the big chains paid for her beef. Pasture-raised, 100% grass-fed, regenerative. They priced it the same as feedlot meat. Rachel knew the system was broken.

sections.heading: From paddock to pick-up, without the middlemen
sections.body:
Rachel became a key partner and supplier to FarmThru. Her farm in Kempsey now sends 100% grass-fed beef straight to families through FarmThru's Brookvale hub.

No wholesaler takes a cut. No cold-storage facility holds her beef for weeks. Families order online, then collect from the hub. The beef is days old, not weeks. And Rachel earns a fairer price for every kilo.

She is not the only farmer who made this choice. Bundarra Farm supplies pork. Collins Wild Caught brings in wild-caught Alaskan salmon. Paris Creek provides biodynamic dairy. Each one is a named farm you can look up. Sydney families collect from the Brookvale hub.

sections.heading: Now you can be part of what they are building
sections.body:
FarmThru is launching an equity crowdfunding campaign on Birchal in mid-2026. For the first time, you can own a share of the grocery store these farmers sell through.

Minimum investment: $50. Cap: $10,000 per investor. A $5 refundable deposit secures VIP access with first-in priority, founder updates, and an exclusive Q&A.

This is an early-stage business. You could lose some or all of your investment. The full disclosure document will be on Birchal. We can't promise returns — early-stage investing carries real risk.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: This is a pre-registration page for an upcoming equity crowdfunding campaign. No money is being raised at this stage. Any future offer will be made via a disclosure document on Birchal. This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice before making any investment decision.

creative_brief: Hero: Rachel on her farm in Kempsey, natural light, cattle grazing behind her. Below-fold: timeline visual from bushfires to regenerative rebuild to FarmThru partnership. Product close-ups of grass-fed cuts. Brookvale hub collection shot. Documentary-style photography. Warm, grounded.
